title: The One With Joeys New Girlfriend

jokes.text: Ross enters with a clearly visible black eye, prompting immediate visual comedy
jokes.setup: Ross enters what should be a normal social situation
jokes.punchline: He has an unexplained black eye that's immediately visible
jokes.explanation: Solid visual setup that creates immediate intrigue and comedy through unexplained physical evidence of mishap

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Hey, what's up?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Ross! What happened to your eye?!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What? Nothing happened.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Dude, you have a massive black eye.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I do not!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Ross, I can see it from here.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Fine! I fell... I fell down the stairs.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Which stairs?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: The... the ones at the museum.

jokes.text: Multiple characters asking 'What happened to your eye?' in overlapping dialogue
jokes.setup: Ross's black eye is visible and concerning
jokes.punchline: Everyone asks the same obvious question simultaneously
jokes.explanation: Natural group reaction that feels authentic to how friends would respond to an injured friend

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What happened to your eye?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What happened to your eye?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: What happened to your eye?

jokes.text: Ross explains he got the black eye from a hockey puck at a Rangers game
jokes.setup: Friends are concerned about Ross's dramatic black eye
jokes.punchline: Mundane sports-related accident explanation deflates the drama
jokes.explanation: Effective deflation of expectation - the dramatic visual gets a mundane explanation

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Ross! What happened to your eye?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, you look like you got in a fight with someone way cooler than you.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I got hit with a volleyball.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: A volleyball?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yeah, I was playing in the park and someone spiked it right in my face.

jokes.text: Joey enters with Kathy, introducing her as his new girlfriend
jokes.setup: Joey has been unlucky in relationships
jokes.punchline: He confidently introduces someone as his 'girlfriend' showing rare relationship success
jokes.explanation: Character-driven humor based on Joey's typically unsuccessful dating life making this introduction significant

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey guys, I want you to meet my girlfriend.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Wait, did hell freeze over?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What? I can have a girlfriend!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Joey, that's great! We're happy for you.

jokes.text: Kathy mentions she's an actress, prompting knowing looks between the friends
jokes.setup: Joey's girlfriends often have questionable career claims
jokes.punchline: The friends' silent reaction suggests they've heard this before
jokes.explanation: Excellent use of character history and silent reaction comedy - the friends know Joey's pattern with 'actresses'

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: She's a... uh... she's an actress.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh yeah? What's she been in?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Well, she's mostly doing theater right now. Off-off Broadway.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: How off-off Broadway are we talking?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Washington Square Park off-off Broadway.

jokes.text: Chandler asks what kind of acting Kathy does, with obvious skepticism in his tone
jokes.setup: Kathy claims to be an actress
jokes.punchline: Chandler's skeptical delivery implies he expects it to be something ridiculous
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler sarcasm delivery that plays into the group's shared skepticism about Joey's girlfriends

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: An actress? What, like you've been in things?

jokes.text: Kathy reveals she does children's theater, specifically playing a lion
jokes.setup: Friends expect Joey's girlfriend to have a dubious acting career
jokes.punchline: It's actually legitimate children's theater, but she plays a lion which is oddly specific
jokes.explanation: Subverts expectation nicely - legitimate theater but with absurd specificity that makes it funny rather than impressive

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: So what does Kathy do? Is she an actress?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, she does children's theater.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Children's theater? That's actually pretty legitimate.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Right? And she plays a lion.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: A lion? In what show?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I don't know, but apparently she's really committed to it.

jokes.text: Joey proudly says 'She roars and everything!' with genuine enthusiasm
jokes.setup: Kathy plays a lion in children's theater
jokes.punchline: Joey is genuinely impressed by basic lion behavior, treating roaring as a special skill
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey character moment - his simple enthusiasm for something basic creates genuine humor. High quotability and character integration.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wow, you were amazing! That roar? That was so real!

dialogue.speaker: Kathy
dialogue.line: Thank you! It's all about getting into character.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No, no, I'm serious. How did you even DO that? Where does that sound come from?

dialogue.speaker: Kathy
dialogue.line: It's just... from my throat?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: That's incredible. Have you ever thought about doing a one-woman show? Just you, roaring for two hours?

jokes.text: Ross mentions he should get going to pick up Ben, shifting focus to his parental responsibilities
jokes.setup: Ross is socializing with friends despite his injury
jokes.punchline: Reminder that he has dad duties that take precedence over socializing
jokes.explanation: Mild character humor about Ross's dual role as friend and responsible father

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I know I should be out having fun with you guys, but I really need to get home.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Ben's got soccer practice in the morning, and Emma needs help with her science project.

jokes.text: Monica suggests Ross should wear sunglasses to hide the black eye from Ben
jokes.setup: Ross has a visible black eye and is going to see his young son
jokes.punchline: Monica's practical but slightly ridiculous suggestion to hide it with sunglasses
jokes.explanation: Monica's caretaker personality showing through with impractical but well-meaning advice

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You could wear sunglasses.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Monica, I can't wear sunglasses. It's nighttime.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: So? Wear them anyway. You'll look cool.

jokes.text: Ross points out that wearing sunglasses indoors would be even more suspicious
jokes.setup: Monica suggests sunglasses to hide the black eye
jokes.punchline: Ross logically points out this would create more questions than it solves
jokes.explanation: Good logical comedy - Ross's analytical nature provides reasonable counterpoint that's also funny

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Why don't you just wear sunglasses to cover it up?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Monica, I can't wear sunglasses. It's nighttime.

jokes.text: Chandler makes a joke about Ross looking like a domestic violence victim
jokes.setup: Ross has a black eye and is worried about appearances
jokes.punchline: Chandler makes an inappropriate but typical dark observation
jokes.explanation: Typical Chandler dark humor but relies on sensitive subject matter for shock value rather than clever writing

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Hey, look on the bright side — at least now when you go on a date, she won't be able to see your face.

jokes.text: Cut to Chandler and Joey's apartment where Chandler is awkwardly trying to make conversation with Kathy
jokes.setup: Chandler is alone with Joey's new girlfriend
jokes.punchline: His obvious awkwardness in trying to be friendly without overstepping
jokes.explanation: Good character-driven awkwardness that sets up potential romantic tension storyline

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So, uh, how long have you known Joey?

dialogue.speaker: Kathy
dialogue.line: About three months.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Three months! That's... that's great. He's a great guy. I mean, I've known him longer, so I can really vouch for that. Not that you need me to vouch. You probably know how great he is by now. Which is good.

dialogue.speaker: Kathy
dialogue.line: Thanks...

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Your hair looks nice today, by the way. I'm not hitting on you. Joey's right there. Metaphorically. Not literally right there, but you know what I mean.

jokes.text: Chandler asks Kathy about her lion role and she demonstrates with an actual roar
jokes.setup: Chandler awkwardly asks about her acting
jokes.punchline: Kathy actually roars like a lion in the apartment
jokes.explanation: Effective callback that pays off Joey's earlier enthusiasm while creating absurd apartment moment

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So, uh, how's the acting going?

dialogue.speaker: Kathy
dialogue.line: Oh, it's great! I just landed this amazing role where I play a fierce lioness.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: That sounds... interesting. What does that involve?

dialogue.speaker: Kathy
dialogue.line: Well, let me show you!

dialogue.speaker: Kathy
dialogue.line: ROOOAAARRRRR!

jokes.text: Chandler's stunned reaction to the roar, clearly not expecting it to be so realistic
jokes.setup: Kathy roars like a lion in close quarters
jokes.punchline: Chandler's visible shock at how intense and realistic it sounds
jokes.explanation: Excellent reaction comedy - Chandler's response sells the absurdity of the situation perfectly

dialogue.speaker: Kathy
dialogue.line: ROOOAAARRR!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh. My. God.

jokes.text: Kathy mentions that children sometimes cry when she roars
jokes.setup: Kathy is proud of her realistic lion performance
jokes.punchline: She casually mentions traumatizing children as a side effect
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy twist - takes the innocent children's theater concept and reveals an unintended consequence

dialogue.speaker: Kathy
dialogue.line: I'm really proud of my lion performance. It's so realistic.

dialogue.speaker: Kathy
dialogue.line: The only downside is I've traumatized a few children, but that's a small price to pay for authenticity.

jokes.text: Chandler sarcastically comments 'That's the goal in children's theater'
jokes.setup: Kathy mentions making children cry with her roaring
jokes.punchline: Chandler's dry observation about the opposite being the actual goal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler delivery - economical, character-appropriate, and genuinely witty observation about the irony

dialogue.speaker: Kathy
dialogue.line: I make children cry with my roaring.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, I make children laugh by leaving.

jokes.text: Joey enters and immediately asks what they're talking about, sensing tension
jokes.setup: Chandler and Kathy were having an intimate conversation
jokes.punchline: Joey's oblivious but possessive entrance breaks the mood
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Joey's territorial instincts despite his generally trusting nature

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey! What's going on in here?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Joey, we're just talking.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Well, talking is my thing with Kathy. So if anyone's gonna talk to her, it's gonna be me.

jokes.text: Chandler awkwardly explains they were discussing her roaring technique
jokes.setup: Joey asks what they were talking about
jokes.punchline: Chandler's explanation sounds potentially suggestive despite being innocent
jokes.explanation: Mild innuendo humor that plays on the awkwardness of the situation without being too obvious

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What were you guys talking about?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh, we were just... well, Monica was on top of me, and I was helping her... move the couch.

jokes.text: Cut to Monica's apartment where she's obsessively organizing already-clean items
jokes.setup: Monica appears to be doing normal cleaning
jokes.punchline: She's reorganizing things that are already perfectly organized
jokes.explanation: Classic Monica obsessive-compulsive behavior that's immediately recognizable and character-appropriate

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I'm just moving these three cans so they're in perfect alphabetical order. Alphabetical by what? By how much I love them.

jokes.text: Rachel enters and asks Monica what she's doing, Monica claims she's 'straightening'
jokes.setup: Monica is clearly over-organizing already perfect space
jokes.punchline: She uses the mild term 'straightening' for her obvious obsessive behavior
jokes.explanation: Good character humor showing Monica's self-awareness and rationalization of her compulsive behavior

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I'm just straightening things up a little.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Monica, everything is already straight.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, it could be straighter.

jokes.text: Rachel points out that Monica straightened those same items yesterday
jokes.setup: Monica claims she's just straightening things
jokes.punchline: Rachel calls out that this is repeat behavior, highlighting the obsession
jokes.explanation: Effective straight-man response that exposes Monica's behavior as genuinely compulsive rather than practical

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I'm just straightening things.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Monica, you did this yesterday. And the day before that. You have a problem.

jokes.text: Monica defensively explains that things get 'unstraight' throughout the day
jokes.setup: Rachel questions Monica's need to re-straighten things
jokes.punchline: Monica invents the word 'unstraight' to justify her behavior
jokes.explanation: Clever character-based wordplay that shows Monica's logical mind trying to rationalize irrational behavior

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Monica, why do you keep straightening things that are already straight?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: They're not straight, they're unstraight.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: That's not a word.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: It is now.

jokes.text: Rachel gives Monica a look that clearly says 'you're crazy' without speaking
jokes.setup: Monica just made up the word 'unstraight' with complete sincerity
jokes.punchline: Rachel's wordless reaction conveys exactly what the audience is thinking
jokes.explanation: Excellent silent comedy - Rachel serves as audience surrogate with perfect non-verbal reaction

jokes.text: Cut to Ross at his ex-wife's apartment, awkwardly explaining his black eye
jokes.setup: Ross needs to explain his injury to his ex-wife
jokes.punchline: His awkward over-explanation makes a simple accident sound suspicious
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows how Ross's tendency to over-explain makes innocent situations seem guilty

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Okay, so I fell down the stairs. But it wasn't like I was pushed or anything. I just... I missed a step. My foot went one way, and my body went another way, and then gravity did its thing.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Ross, you tripped?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, yes, but the point is, I was carrying a box at the time, so my spatial awareness was compromised. And the lighting on the landing was dim. Very dim. Almost intentionally dim, which now that I think about it is suspicious, but that's not the point.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Why are you explaining this like you committed a crime?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I'm not! I'm just saying, if anyone asks, there were no witnesses, and I was wearing clothes that blended into the staircase pattern, so really, it's almost like it didn't happen.

jokes.text: Carol (his ex-wife) sarcastically asks if the hockey puck 'jumped up and hit him'
jokes.setup: Ross is giving an elaborate explanation for a hockey injury
jokes.punchline: Carol's sarcastic response implies she doesn't believe the accident story
jokes.explanation: Sharp sarcastic response that plays into the absurdity of Ross's defensive explanation

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: So I was going for the puck, right? And this guy, he comes out of nowhere, and his stick just clips my shoulder, and then I fall into the boards, and somehow my arm gets twisted underneath me...

dialogue.speaker: Carol
dialogue.line: Uh-huh. And this all happened... at the gym?

jokes.text: Ross literally explains that yes, the puck did bounce up and hit him, missing Carol's sarcasm entirely
jokes.setup: Carol makes a sarcastic comment about the hockey puck
jokes.punchline: Ross takes it literally and earnestly confirms that's exactly what happened
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross character moment - his literal-mindedness creates comedy by completely missing social cues

dialogue.speaker: Carol
dialogue.line: Oh sure, Ross, because a hockey puck just flew through our window for no reason at all.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, actually, that's exactly what happened. The puck had a velocity of approximately 80 miles per hour, and given the angle of trajectory and the structural weaknesses in the window frame...

jokes.text: Ben runs in and immediately asks daddy why he has a 'boo-boo' on his face
jokes.setup: Ross was worried about how to explain his black eye to Ben
jokes.punchline: Ben notices immediately and uses innocent child language that makes it sound worse
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to Ross's earlier concerns, with child's innocent observation making the situation more awkward

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Dad, what happened to your face? Did you get in a fight?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No, no, I just... I fell.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: You fell? On what, a fist?

jokes.text: Ross kneels down and gives Ben an age-appropriate explanation about accidents at hockey games
jokes.setup: Ben innocently asks about daddy's injury
jokes.punchline: Ross's sweet, patient father mode contrasts with his earlier defensive explanations to adults
jokes.explanation: Nice character contrast showing Ross can be clear and reassuring with Ben while being awkward with adults

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Daddy, what happened to your arm?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, buddy, Daddy had a little accident.

jokes.text: Ben asks if Ross is going to die, escalating the situation dramatically
jokes.setup: Ross gives a calm explanation about his minor sports injury
jokes.punchline: Ben jumps to the most extreme possible conclusion about mortality
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation comedy - child logic that takes minor injury to maximum dramatic conclusion

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: It's just a minor sprain. I'll be fine in a few days.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Dad, you're dying aren't you?

jokes.text: Ross frantically reassures Ben while Carol watches with obvious amusement
jokes.setup: Ben thinks Ross might die from a black eye
jokes.punchline: Ross panics while Carol enjoys watching him squirm
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Ross's panic and Carol's enjoyment of his parental predicament

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Dad, you look like you might die.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What?! No, I'm fine! It's just a black eye!

dialogue.speaker: Carol
dialogue.line: Oh, this is good.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What do you mean 'this is good'? I might have a serious injury!

dialogue.speaker: Carol
dialogue.line: I know. Isn't it great?

jokes.text: Back at Central Perk, Joey is telling Gunther about Kathy's roaring abilities
jokes.setup: Joey is proud of his girlfriend's unique talent
jokes.punchline: He's earnestly bragging to Gunther about something most people would find odd
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Joey's continued enthusiasm and his tendency to overshare with inappropriate audiences

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Dude, my girlfriend can lick her own elbow.

dialogue.speaker: Gunther
dialogue.line: That's... that's great, Joey.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I know, right? She's incredible!

jokes.text: Gunther's deadpan response suggests he's not impressed by roaring skills
jokes.setup: Joey enthusiastically describes Kathy's roaring talent
jokes.punchline: Gunther's flat, unimpressed reaction deflates Joey's enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Classic Gunther deadpan delivery that serves as reality check to Joey's over-excitement

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: She's amazing! She roars, Gunther. Like, full-on roaring. It's incredible!

dialogue.speaker: Gunther
dialogue.line: Yeah. That's... great.

jokes.text: Chandler enters looking troubled and sits with an exaggerated sigh
jokes.setup: Chandler appears to join the coffee shop conversation normally
jokes.punchline: His dramatic entrance and sigh suggest internal conflict about Kathy
jokes.explanation: Setup for romantic tension storyline, showing Chandler's internal struggle through physical comedy

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: His dramatic entrance and sigh suggest internal conflict about Kathy

jokes.text: Joey obliviously asks Chandler what he thinks about Kathy, putting him on the spot
jokes.setup: Chandler is clearly struggling with feelings about Kathy
jokes.punchline: Joey innocently asks for his opinion, creating maximum awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe setup - Joey's obliviousness creates perfect awkward situation for Chandler

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: So Chandler, what do you think of Kathy?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What? I... she's... I mean, she seems nice. Why are you asking me this?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I don't know, man. You've been staring at her all night.

jokes.text: Chandler awkwardly tries to give a neutral response about Kathy being 'nice'
jokes.setup: Joey asks Chandler's opinion about Kathy when Chandler has romantic feelings
jokes.punchline: Chandler's painfully safe and underwhelming response reveals his discomfort
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Chandler's attempt to hide his true feelings with bland politeness

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: So what do you think of Kathy?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: She's... she's great. Really great. I mean, she's got hair, and... eyes. And she speaks words. In sentences, even.

jokes.text: Joey pushes for more details, asking what specifically Chandler likes about her
jokes.setup: Chandler gives a minimal, safe response about Kathy
jokes.punchline: Joey's persistence forces Chandler deeper into uncomfortable territory
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of awkwardness - Joey's innocent enthusiasm makes Chandler's situation worse

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Come on, what do you really think about Kathy?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: She's great. I think she's really great.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, but like, what do you REALLY think?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well... I mean, she's very... attractive.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh come on!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Fine! Fine! She's... she's incredibly attractive. And I think about her... more than I should.

jokes.text: Chandler mentions Kathy's intelligence, then catches himself sounding too enthusiastic
jokes.setup: Joey wants specific compliments about his girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Chandler starts to genuinely praise her, then realizes he's revealing too much
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Chandler's internal conflict - his genuine feelings slip out before he can censor himself

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Just tell her something nice about herself.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: She's got great... eyes. Yeah, really beautiful eyes.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Come on, you can do better than that.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Okay, okay. She's smart, she's funny, she's got this amazing laugh, and when she talks about things she cares about, her whole face just lights up—

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Wait. Why am I saying this? How long have I been looking at her?

jokes.text: Joey beams with pride and thanks Chandler for the compliment about his girlfriend
jokes.setup: Chandler reluctantly compliments Kathy's intelligence
jokes.punchline: Joey's grateful response highlights the ironic situation - he's thanking his roommate for falling for his girlfriend
jokes.explanation: Strong ironic comedy - Joey's innocent gratitude underscores the complicated emotional situation he's unaware of

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Okay, Kathy, you're really smart.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Thanks, man!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I wasn't talking to you.

jokes.text: Chandler excuses himself with obvious discomfort, claiming he needs to get home
jokes.setup: Chandler is trapped in an awkward conversation about Kathy
jokes.punchline: His sudden need to escape the situation is transparently about his emotional discomfort
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Chandler's flight response when emotions get too complicated

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh, uh, hey, I just remembered I have to... um... go do that thing. You know, the thing I mentioned? Yeah, that's happening right now, and I really need to be there for it.

jokes.text: Final scene: Ross enters Central Perk with an even more dramatic black eye than before
jokes.setup: Ross had a black eye at the beginning of the episode
jokes.punchline: His black eye is now noticeably worse, suggesting more accidents occurred
jokes.explanation: Good callback escalation - visual comedy that suggests Ross's day got progressively worse

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What? What are you all staring at?

jokes.text: All friends simultaneously ask 'What happened NOW?' in perfect unison
jokes.setup: Ross appears with an even worse black eye
jokes.punchline: The group's synchronized response mirrors their earlier reaction but with escalated concern
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that escalates the original joke - perfect group timing creates strong comedic payoff

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Ross! What happened?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Your eye looks even worse!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Dude, you look like you lost a fight with a very angry pillow.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: How did you do that to yourself again?

jokes.text: Ross sheepishly explains that Ben wanted to play hockey in the apartment
jokes.setup: Ross's black eye is dramatically worse than before
jokes.punchline: He got injured playing indoor hockey with his young son
jokes.explanation: Great callback that connects Ross's earlier injury with Ben's worry, showing Ross's dedication as a father led to more injury

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I know what you're all thinking. Yes, my eye looks worse. But you should see the other guy.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What, Ben?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: He's five!

jokes.text: Chandler delivers the final line: 'Maybe next time he'll want to play boxing'
jokes.setup: Ross got injured playing indoor hockey with Ben
jokes.punchline: Chandler suggests an even more dangerous father-son activity
jokes.explanation: Perfect episode-ending joke - Chandler's characteristic sarcasm that escalates the physical comedy premise to its logical extreme

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You know what? Next time, you two should do something even more dangerous together.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Like what?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Parenting.

tmdb_overview: Chandler hits on a woman at Central Perk, not realizing she is Joey's new girlfriend. He soon realizes he is very attracted to, possibly in love, with her. Ross and Rachel play the jealousy game in their relationships. And Phoebe desperately tries to catch a cold after discovering that her voice is sexier and her singing better when she has phlegm in her throat.
insight: Relationship drama dilutes humor; 44 jokes spread thin across romantic tension.
